item.title: England thrash Flying Fijians 73-8 in emphatic Test victory
item.description: England delivered a ruthless attacking display to beat the FIJI Water Flying Fijians 73-8 in their Test match, running in 11 tries to secure a comprehensive victory. Marcus Smith opened the scoring for England before Jamie George, Guy Pepper and Seb Atkinson also crossed in the first half as England took a commanding 35-3 lead into the break.
item.content:encoded:
England delivered a ruthless attacking display to beat the FIJI Water Flying Fijians 73-8 in their Test match, running in 11 tries to secure a comprehensive victory.

Marcus Smith opened the scoring for England before Jamie George, Guy Pepper and Seb Atkinson also crossed in the first half as England took a commanding 35-3 lead into the break.

Fiji's discipline proved costly, with Levani Botia sent to the sin bin in the 27th minute for a dangerous tackle in the air.

The Flying Fijians suffered another major setback just before half-time when scrum-half Simione Kuruvoli was shown a red card following a TMO review after he was ruled to have kicked England hooker Ellis Genge.

Fiji also had a try ruled out following the incident, leaving them trailing heavily at the interval.

Captain Tevita Ikanivere scored Fiji's only try early in the second half after sustained pressure close to England's line, but Caleb Muntz was unable to convert.

England continued to dominate after the break despite having one try ruled out for a double movement.

Immanuel Feyi-Waboso, Henry Slade, Noah Caluori and Henry Pollock, who scored two tries, added to England's tally as the visitors pulled away.

Marcus Smith and Fin Smith were accurate from the kicking tee, converting most of England's tries.

In other results, the All Blacks thumped Italy 47-17, France defeated Australia 42-26, Ireland beat Japan 36-20, and South Africa defeated Scotland 42-28.

item.title: Two dead, including a 10-year-old child, in tragic Laucala Beach blaze
item.description: A devastating fire at Laucala Beach this morning has claimed the lives of two people, including a 10-year-old child. The National Fire Authority has confirmed the deaths to fijivillage News, and says investigations into the cause of the fire are ongoing.
item.content:encoded:
A devastating fire at Laucala Beach this morning has claimed the lives of two people, including a 10-year-old child.

The National Fire Authority has confirmed the deaths to fijivillage News, and says investigations into the cause of the fire are ongoing.

The blaze broke out at around 8am before rapidly spreading, destroying the residential property and extending to nearby commercial buildings.

They say the Police Forensic Team has recovered the bodies of the two victims, which have since been taken to the hospital for further examination.

The tragic incident has left the community in shock, with many gathering near the scene as emergency crews continue their work.

Stay with us for more updates as this story develops.

item.title: Serious Organised Crime officer says drugs were found on table and in bedroom at Rewa St house
item.description: Serious Organised Crime Department officer Josua Cakausese says illicit drugs were found on a table and in another bedroom at a house at Lot 134 Rewa Street on the 23rd of September, 2025. While giving evidence during the hearing, Cakausese says when they arrived at the scene, the house was locked and a Palauan Islander was inside.
item.content:encoded:
Serious Organised Crime Department officer Josua Cakausese says illicit drugs were found on a table and in another bedroom at a house at Lot 134 Rewa Street on the 23rd of September, 2025.

While giving evidence during the hearing, Cakausese says when they arrived at the scene, the house was locked and a Palauan Islander was inside.

He says when Ginna Choi arrived, he explained to them the reason for the search.

During cross-examination, defence lawyer Jerry Dinati questioned Cakausese on whether Choi was found in possession of any drugs at Lot 170, and Cakausese agreed that she was not.

He also agreed that they searched the house without a search warrant.

However, during re-examination by State lawyer Eoghn Samisoni, Cakausese explained that Section 22 of the Illicit Drugs Control Act allows them to do so.

Yu Yang Feng, Minghui Dai and Zhou Lan are each charged with one count of unlawful possession of illicit drugs for allegedly being found with 39.509 grams of methamphetamine.

Lan is also charged with possession of property suspected to be proceeds of a crime for allegedly being found with FJD$7,180, USD$60, 1,520 in Hong Kong currency and 1,600 Chinese yuan.

Feng is also charged with unlawful supply of illicit drugs.

It is alleged that on the 23rd of September, 2025, without lawful authority, Feng engaged in dealings with other persons for the transport and supply of methamphetamine.

He is also charged with being in possession of property suspected to be proceeds of a crime.

Feng was allegedly found with $906.25 in cash.

Choi and Peniasi Delai are charged with one count of unlawful possession of illicit drugs for allegedly being found with 0.345 grams of methamphetamine.

item.title: Concerns raised over incorrect road markings and illegal parking in Nausori Town
item.description: Concerns have been raised over alleged incorrect road markings, lack of road signs and illegal parking in parts of Nausori Town.A

item.title: GCC wants a constitution developed by the people, for the people

item.description: Villagers of Tamavua will soon have a state-of-the-art evacuation centre following the groundbreaking ceremony for the $1.78 milli

item.title: Naivalurua commends Beqa graduates for commitment to building a safer and stronger Fiji
item.description: More than 10 participants successfully completed training under the Solesolevaki ni Veitaqomaki initiative at Raviravi Village on Beqa Island and the Minister for Policing, Ioane Naivalurua has acknowledged their commitment. Naivalurua , who officiated as the chief guest at the graduation ceremony, thanked the graduates for their dedication and contribution towards building a better nation for fut
item.content:encoded:
More than 10 participants successfully completed training under the Solesolevaki ni Veitaqomaki initiative at Raviravi Village on Beqa Island and the Minister for Policing, Ioane Naivalurua has acknowledged their commitment.

Naivalurua , who officiated as the chief guest at the graduation ceremony, thanked the graduates for their dedication and contribution towards building a better nation for future generations.

He says the government recognises the commitment shown by the participants and their willingness to play a role in creating safer and stronger communities.

The Minister says the purpose of the initiative is to build, progress, sustain and achieve prosperity for the future.

Naivalurua stressed that this responsibility must be shared individually, collectively as a vanua, and as a nation to ensure a secure and prosperous Fiji.

The Solesolevaki ni Veitaqomaki initiative aims to strengthen community participation in promoting safety, security and national development.

The graduates successfully completed nationally recognised maritime training aimed at enhancing maritime safety, strengthening technical skills, and building the capacity of island communities to safely operate and maintain vessels while improving their resilience and self-reliance.

item.description: The Suva High Court has refused an application by Imran Khan, also known as King Khan, to vacate his guilty plea on one count of c
